sources_checked.limitation: Homepage confirms restaurant identity and address (125 first ave, new york, ny 10003) but contains no menu content; links to a separate 'food' menu page.
Menu page confirms hours/location/identity but embeds the actual food menu as an external link to a Canva-hosted site rather than inline content.

Canva export page returned an empty JS shell via mcp__workspace__web_fetch; required Claude-in-Chrome rendering (navigate + get_page_text) to extract visible text. Once rendered, produced a complete, dated (06.29.2026) dinner menu with prices, matching a modern small-plates/Korean-Spanish fine dining format. Only the 'food' menu tier was fetched; the separate 'beverage' link was not fetched (out of scope per instructions to consolidate beverages into one record, and no beverage-specific dish detail was needed).

notes: Menu appears to be an a la carte small-plates format rather than a fixed tasting menu; no restaurant-labeled section headers were present in the rendered text so menu_section groupings (Small Plates, Mains, Sides, Desserts) were inferred from item type/ordering. No explicit allergen disclosures were present on the menu beyond the standard raw/undercooked food safety disclaimer; all gluten flags are inferred (likely), none declared. No peanut or tree nut ingredients were named anywhere on the menu, so no peanut/tree nut flags were generated for any dish.
